contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC Asia Taichung Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Maximize number of color segments after limited length-k flips.
Writeup transforms the string by adjacent XOR differences, splits by residue modulo k, and merges per-class DP by knapsack.
Alternative writeup emphasizes difference transform, independent residue classes, and grouped knapsack optimization.

annotation.summary: After at most m flips of length k in a binary string, maximize the number of color segments.
annotation.constraints: n around 3000, so O(nm) DP is acceptable but naive state over the whole string is not.
annotation.core_idea: The number of segments equals the count of adjacent differences. Flipping [i,i+k) only toggles two boundary difference positions, so positions with the same index modulo k become independent chains where an operation toggles adjacent bits. Compute for each residue class the best gain for each number of operations, then combine classes with group knapsack.
annotation.complexity: O(nm) after splitting by residue classes.

solution_variants.name: difference-class-dp-knapsack
solution_variants.summary: Convert to adjacent-difference bits, solve each modulo-k chain by DP, and combine operation budgets.

tags.evidence: Each residue class is solved with a DP over position, operations, and last state.
Per-class DP tables are combined by distributing the global operation budget.
tags.description: Dynamic programming that combines item/group choices under a budget or capacity.
tags.created_reason: Budget-distribution DP is common enough to deserve a searchable subtag.

tags.evidence: The key insight is converting segment count to adjacent XOR differences so a range flip changes two positions.
tags.description: Transform an array/string to adjacent differences so range operations become endpoint operations.
tags.created_reason: Difference transforms are a recurring trick distinct from prefix-sum conversion.

tags.evidence: The grouping is by residue modulo k rather than divisor; this tag should not be matched by default.
